subject: Adaptive generator balance — difficulty-based ratio shifting (feeds cognitive tempo)

body.context: Session 91 implemented adaptive generator balance in compute-generator-balance.py. The difficulty signal that drives balance ratio also feeds the cognitive-tempo tier selection. Sending the updated script for meshd integration — you decide whether to call it as Python or port to Go.

what_changed.summary: Replaced fixed ~2:1 creative/evaluative target with adaptive function: target_ratio = 4.0 - (2.5 * difficulty), clamped [1.5, 4.0]. Difficulty derived from commit message keyword analysis.

difficulty_estimation.method: Keyword heuristic on commit messages, three tiers weighted 1.0/0.5/0.0
difficulty_estimation.high_markers: architect, theor, deriv, formal, invariant, governance, ontolog, epistem, consciousness, refactor, redesign, adjudicat, knock-on, convergence, psychometric, bifactor, calibrat, conservation law
difficulty_estimation.moderate_markers: spec, protocol, schema, migration, evaluation, analysis, integration, mapping, grounding, literature, model, taxonomy, framework
difficulty_estimation.low_markers: ACK, sync, commit, push, merge, transport, heartbeat, status, rename, typo, fix:, cleanup, format, lint, update:, bump

adaptive_function.grounding: Snell et al. (2024) LLM test-time compute; Paulus & Nijstad (2003) brainstorming; project empirical baseline (5,972 commits, 1.89:1 observed)

meshd_integration.option_a: Subprocess call: python3 scripts/compute-generator-balance.py --session-id N (current interface, zero changes needed)
meshd_integration.option_b: Port estimate_session_difficulty() and adaptive_target_ratio() to Go — two small functions, ~40 lines. The difficulty signal feeds cognitive-tempo tier selection directly.
meshd_integration.recommendation: Start with option A. Port to Go only if subprocess latency matters for real-time tier switching.

body.connection_to_tempo: The difficulty score that drives generator balance also informs model tier selection: high difficulty sessions warrant opus (more evaluation needed = deeper reasoning needed). The estimate_session_difficulty() function provides a session-level signal complementing the per-message task_complexity from cognitive-tempo-model §3.2.

claims.text: Adaptive function validated on Session 90 (difficulty 0.297, target 3.26:1, actual 2.55:1) and Session 84 (difficulty 0.361, target 3.1:1, actual 0.65:1)
claims.confidence_basis: Direct computation on historical commit data

claims.text: Session difficulty estimation uses only commit message text — no LLM cost, no external dependencies

action_gate.gate_condition: none — informational delivery, ops decides integration path

epistemic_flags: Difficulty markers represent heuristic keyword lists, not validated complexity measures
Linear interpolation between endpoints assumes monotonic difficulty-ratio relationship — convex or concave functions may fit better but lack grounding data
Session-level difficulty averages across commits — within-session difficulty variance not captured
